Mar 6, 2026, 4:00 PM EST

Recent Developments

Updated 2 minutes ago
29 minutes ago
Oil Spike Drives Sharp US Market Selloff
US equities declined Friday as West Texas Intermediate crude surged above ninety dollars per barrel, marking approximately a thirty-five percent weekly gain. The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped four hundred fifty-three points to close at forty-seven thousand five hundred one. The S&P five hundred fell one point three three percent while the Nasdaq Composite declined one point five nine percent.
29 minutes ago
February Payrolls Contract Ninety-Two Thousand Jobs
Nonfarm payrolls fell by ninety-two thousand in February, significantly below expectations, while unemployment rose to four point four percent. The labor market contraction marks the third decline in five months, heightening concerns about economic softening amid persistent inflation pressures.
Mar 5, 2026
BlackRock Limits Withdrawals From Private Credit Fund
BlackRock capped withdrawals at five percent from its twenty-six billion dollar HPS Corporate lending fund after shareholders requested nine point three percent of assets. The move sparked contagion fears across private credit markets, sending BlackRock shares down seven percent and pressuring peers.
